title: The 7 Highest-Yielding S&P 500 Stocks Could Be Huge ‘Strong Buy' Winners for the Second Half of 2023
text: Investors love dividend stocks. They not only provide dependable income, but they also give investors a great opportunity for solid total return.

title: Simon Property Group: Rethinking My Valuation Of The Stock (Rating Downgrade)
text: Using my own experience with Simon Property Group's valuation as an example, I discuss the challenges of human thinking and its impact on financial analysis. I highlight the importance of considering changing discount rates and growth rates in valuation, and how these factors can significantly impact the perceived value of a stock. My previous valuation of Simon Property Group stands revised, now estimating a price range of $140 to $170, which still offers a solid upside potential for investors.

title: Is Simon Property Group Stock a Buy?
text: Simon Property Group is a fundamentally sound business. The company's market-crushing dividend looks safe.

title: Is It Wise to Retain Simon Property (SPG) Stock Right Now?
text: Simon Property (SPG) is poised to benefit from its portfolio of premium assets, a focus on omnichannel retailing and strategic buyouts, though higher e-commerce adoption and high rates are worrisome.

title: Ranking 10 REITs From Highest Quality To Lowest Quality
text: Ranking 10 REITs solely based on quality and long-term viability. Many of these REITs are leaders within their particular sector, but not all are intriguing in terms of valuations. Only 1 REIT on the list is rated as a "PREFER NOT TO OWN" stock.

title: 2 Quality Stocks Politicians Have Been Buying
text: Politicians are at the forefront of new regulation approval, policy changes and have a general feel for the headwinds facing certain industries. Therefore, they are deemed to be people of influence.

title: Could This Be One of the Cheapest Stocks in the Market? Its Leaders Think So
text: This real estate company's leadership just made the case that it's extremely cheap right now.

title: Here's How Simon Property Group Can Afford Its 6.6% Dividend Yield
text: Simon Property Group is benefiting from strong demand for retail space. The REIT's dividend is well-covered.

title: Simon (SPG) Bolsters Shopping Experience With Razer Collab
text: Simon Property (SPG) announces collaboration with Razer to bring award-winning gaming products to five of its shopping centers, bolstering the in-person shopping experience for customers.

title: Insider Buying Supports a Bullish Case for Simon Property Group
text: There are many reasons why investors will sell a stock. We're only human after all.

title: Deep Value Moments
text: One month ago, I was privileged to speak at the London Value Investor Conference (LVIC). Fortunately for us, Ben Inker, co-head of asset allocation at Grantham Mayo Van Otterloo (GMO), kicked off the session with a presentation arguing that deep-value stocks were historically attractive relative to all forms of growth stocks, and very compelling versus quality value stocks.

title: Simon Property Group: Interesting Undervaluation Levels Once Again
text: It's been some time since I last reviewed Simon Property Group, Inc. A big part of my position was established at double-digit lows 1-2 years ago. For that reason, I've been hesitant to expand upon Simon Property Group stock at prices above $110, even though $110 may be a good price from a long-term perspective. However, right now, the company is touching triple-digit lows and skirting double-digit pricing. I believe the time has come to revisit SPG.

title: Tech Companies Have Lots of News for Their Investors
Simon Property Vs. Macerich: What's The Best Mall REIT To Buy Today?
text: Mall REITs are today hated and deeply discounted. We think that Simon Property Group and Macerich are both attractive. But which one is the best buying opportunity today?

title: Leverage Is a Lead Weight On This High-Yielder's Recovery
text: Macerich owns a collection of high-performing malls. The stock has a large 7.2% dividend yield.

title: Simon (SPG) Boosts Northgate Station With New Residence Inn
text: Simon's (SPG) landmark venture represents the latest transformation in mixed-use development, marking the first hotel within the Northgate Station complex.
